executionPolicy.shippingGatewaySubstrate: NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter (in-process HTTP.sys, v0.9.0+); the legacy external gateway retired

phases.label: Repository baseline and ADR lock
phases.objective: Freeze the new architecture in repo docs before code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: New ADR states MCOS is not direct AI executor
MCP Gateway and LAN discovery are first-class modules
Current provider/client/auth semantics documented before change
validation: No code edits unless needed for docs index

phases.label: Remove provider-era direct AI integration
phases.objective: Remove or quarantine direct AI-provider execution and provider assignment paths.
deliverables: Docs updated from provider execution to external client onboarding
acceptanceCriteria: No ChatGPT/Codex/Claude/Grok direct execution path remains in MCOS
Client model is external-agent oriented
Tests updated for semantic reset

phases.label: MCP Gateway spike behind IMcpGateway
phases.objective: Add replaceable MCP Gateway adapter behind the IMcpGateway interface and validate it against the first logical-endpoint registration path.
deliverables: First logical MCP endpoint registration path
acceptanceCriteria: MCOS exposes one gateway MCP URL
Adapter implements IMcpGateway cleanly
Adapter can be replaced later
No worker clone is exposed directly as public gateway server

phases.label: Bonjour-style LAN discovery and beacon correction
phases.objective: Advertise MCOS gateway via DNS-SD/mDNS and normalized discovery JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: DNS-SD TXT fields include gateway/config/governance paths
Discovery JSON matches schema
Beacon is gateway-first not provider-first

phases.label: Model-specific onboarding profiles
phases.objective: Generate configuration profiles for Claude Code, Codex, Grok, ChatGPT connector-edge, and generic MCP clients.
deliverables: Companion utility plan if auto-apply is not feasible
acceptanceCriteria: Every profile uses one gateway MCP URL
Profiles state authRequired=false for LAN gateway
ChatGPT path is documented as connector-edge/optional where needed
No direct provider credentials collected by MCOS for gateway use

phases.label: CLU/Forsetti governance bundle distribution
phases.objective: Deliver platform-specific governance bundles and CLU decision behavior to clients.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows governance bundle works
macOS/iOS paths represented
CLU profile served during onboarding
Forsetti compliance script passes or reports exact blockers

phases.label: Managed MCP/sub-agent worker pools
phases.objective: Turn MCP servers and sub-agents into supervised managed endpoint pools.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP servers and sub-agents use same operational abstraction
Worker process trees are supervised
Health states visible through API
No fake live infrastructure

phases.label: Autoscaling and lease routing
phases.objective: Scale endpoint pools and route new sessions/leases under load.
acceptanceCriteria: Heavy utilization triggers same-type scale-out
Existing stateful sessions drain/stick
New sessions route to scaled instance
Dashboard/API show pool saturation

phases.label: Real-time telemetry model
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard APIs expose real metrics or unavailable states
No fake client CPU/GPU without heartbeat
Warnings/errors stream in real time
Worker pool telemetry includes utilization and waits

phases.label: Tron dashboard realignment
phases.objective: Rebuild dashboard around gateway, clients, workers, telemetry, and activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard no longer centers provider execution
All required real-time telemetry appears
Generic unavailable states are honest
Manual/import setup paths remain visible

phases.label: Windows hardening, CI, packaging, and release gate
phases.objective: Make release validation trustworthy and Windows production behavior explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows build/test/package gate blocks release
Manual dispatch cannot bypass successful same-SHA build
Toolchain discovery avoids hardcoded VS Enterprise path
Release version stamped before configure/build/package
validation: Package script dry run if possible

phases.label: Native gateway evaluation and the in-process HTTP.sys adapter replacement option
phases.objective: Evaluate whether to keep the in-process HTTP.sys adapter or implement native MCOS gateway after spike data.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision is evidence-based
external-substrate limitations documented
Native implementation scope does not break previous phases

phases.label: Native HTTP.sys MCP Gateway substrate (replaces the in-process HTTP.sys adapter)
phases.status: shipped at v0.9.0; NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter retired in the same release
phases.objective: Replace the gateway adapter child process with a Windows-native MCP Gateway implemented inside MCOS in C++ on top of HTTP.sys. Maintainers get a usable gateway out of the box with no external binary to install.
deliverables: HTTP.sys URL Group + Server Session listener bound to mcpGateway.listenHost:listenPort
Path router /mcp and /mcp/{poolId} dispatching through the supervisor lease router
MCP protocol surface: tools/list, tools/call, notifications/initialized
Telemetry wiring into the events ring + GatewayTrafficSnapshot
Retirement of NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter source files at v0.9.0
acceptanceCriteria: MCOS exposes a usable MCP gateway with zero external binary install
MCP initialize handshake succeeds against http://<lan-ip>:8080/mcp returning serverInfo MCOS Native Gateway
tools/list and tools/call route through the supervisor + lease router
Self-test probes for the gateway are green at boot
GET /mcp returns 405 Method Not Allowed (listener alive proof)
GET /health returns 200 with gateway-state JSON
validation: POST http://localhost:8080/mcp jsonrpc:initialize returns MCOS Native Gateway

phases.label: Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the WinUI Shell
phases.status: scheduled (reserved phase-id slot, not yet started); independent of PHASE-12
phases.objective: Move high-rate live visual surfaces in the WinUI Shell from XAML automatic Composition rendering to explicit Direct2D / Win2D drawing -- per-instance telemetry charts at 60Hz, the Tron grid backdrop as a procedural shader, and the activity stream as a flowing scrollable log. WinUI 3 + XAML stays the host; PHASE-13 slots Win2D-rendered surfaces inside the existing tree.
deliverables: CanvasControl per pool card showing CPU + RAM time series at 60Hz
Procedural Tron grid backdrop via HLSL fragment shader
Performance budget: under 0.5ms per frame per converted surface
acceptanceCriteria: All converted surfaces render at vsync-locked 60Hz
XAML re-layout no longer triggered by per-snapshot data updates on those surfaces
Maintainer-perceived shell sluggishness eliminated for live visuals
Multi-DPI displays render the Tron grid sharply
validation: Maintainer visual confirmation across primary + secondary monitors
ctest snapshot tests for non-Win2D regressions

phases.label: Comprehensive diagnostics module with persistent store and maintainer export
phases.objective: Replace per-component scattered logging with a single Diagnostics Service that owns an in-memory ring + SQLite persistent store, receives every error/warning/event from every MCOS subsystem (including the existing boot self-test snapshot), exposes HTTP/MCP queries, and surfaces in the WinUI Shell + browser dashboard with native Markdown/JSON export through a Save-As file picker.
deliverables: DiagnosticsTypes.h (severity enum, record struct, query, export format)
appendEvent / appendTelemetry shim routing into the service
WinUI Shell DiagnosticsSectionControl with FileSavePicker-driven Export Markdown + Export JSON buttons
Browser dashboard Diagnostics tab + Export buttons (Blob download)
Self-test snapshot persisted on every boot
Tests for store CRUD, query filters, ring eviction, export rendering, HTTP routes
acceptanceCriteria: Stop+restart preserves the events table with session_id intact
Boot self-test snapshot lands in the store and is queryable
All current appendEvent call sites also appear in the central store
GET /api/diagnostics/export?format=markdown returns rendered text/markdown
Claude Code can call mcos_diagnostics_export_markdown via the MCP plugin and receive the rendered doc
Maintainer clicks Export Markdown in the shell, native Windows file picker opens, file is written to the chosen path, status TextBlock shows the full path
Maintainer clicks Export JSON in the browser, browser download dialog appears with the suggested filename
If SQLite DB is unavailable, DiagnosticsService::report returns ok=false with a reason; runtime continues; summary() includes storeUnavailable; no fake-success state
scripts/check-mastercontrol-forsetti.ps1 passes after changes
ctest passes with new tests
phases.approvalGate: Required before MCOS source edits per CLAUDE.md Required execution behavior §6. Maintainer authorizes by replying 'PHASE-14 approved' (optionally naming a starting slice A-E).
